step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the consensus. The continuous nature of ion exchange supports ongoing remineralization processes which help maintain oral health over time. Additionally, since fluoride ions from external sources can be incorporated into the enamel structure even later in life, this further underscores why ion exchange does indeed continue throughout adult life.

Answer: D Your analysis correctly reinforces the idea that ion exchange continues throughout life due to its role in maintaining and improving dental health via remineralization and incorporating beneficial minerals like fluoride. This point about fluoride specifically strengthens the argument for option D being correct.
